L1.area: Image format decoding and pixel transformation pipeline

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in an image format decoder where a vector with a user-provided type parameter is allocated and its length is set via unsafe code before the elements are properly initialized. If a panic occurs during pixel processing before all elements are written, Drop implementations may execute on uninitialized memory, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: HDR (Radiance) image format decoding and pixel transformation
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the HDR image format decoder where a vector with a user-provided type parameter is allocated and its length is set via unsafe code before the elements are properly initialized. If a panic occurs during scanline reading or pixel transformation before all elements are written, Drop implementations may execute on uninitialized memory, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution via a crafted HDR image.

ground_truth.reason: The HDR decoder's image transformation method uses Vec::set_len on an uninitialized vector with a user-provided type parameter. If a panic occurs before initialization completes, Drop runs on uninitialized memory, equivalent to use-after-free.
